Discussed in https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/discussions/30233
Originally posted by **AhmedThahir** November 7, 2024 Feature request: Allow disable refitting of cross-validation estimators (such as LassoCV, RidgeCV) on the full training set after finding the best hyperparameters? Sometimes I only want the optimal hyperparameter and do not want to waste resources on refitting. This is especially important for large datasets.
As @alifa98 has highlighted, this is the relevant code block. https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/fba028b07ed2b4e52dd3719dad0d990837bde28c/sklearn/linear_model/_coordinate_descent.py#L1815 User should be allowed to toggle this behavior. Describe the bug

```
parse_time("202201021230")
 <Time object: scale='utc' format='isot' value=2022-01-02T12:03:00.000>

```

however, 
```
parse_time("20220102123000")
 <Time object: scale='utc' format='isot' value=2022-01-02T12:30:00.000>
```

### To Reproduce

import sunpy

sunpy.map.Map(...)
etc

This PR addresses an issue with the parse_time function where it incorrectly parses time strings with a 4-digit minute and 2-digit second format, leading to the dropping of seconds when the input has 6 digits.

Changes:

- Modified the time parsing regex to preserve leading zeros in minutes and seconds.
- Modified the time parsing regex to handle both 4-digit minutes and 6-digit time formats correctly, ensuring that seconds are retained even when the time string includes the minute and second part in a combined format (e.g., "202201021230" vs "20220102123000").
- Ensured consistent behavior when handling time strings with single-digit minutes/seconds (e.g., "09:05" instead of "9:5").

This fix ensures that both formats are parsed correctly, preserving accurate minute and second #7897 
This PR addresses an issue with the parse_time function where it incorrectly parses time strings with a 4-digit minute and 2-digit second format, leading to the dropping of seconds when the input has 6 digits.

Changes:

- Modified the time parsing regex to preserve leading zeros in minutes and seconds.
- Modified the time parsing regex to handle both 4-digit minutes and 6-digit time formats correctly, ensuring that seconds are retained even when the time string includes the minute and second part in a combined format (e.g., "202201021230" vs "20220102123000").
- Ensured consistent behavior when handling time strings with single-digit minutes/seconds (e.g., "09:05" instead of "9:5").

This fix ensures that both formats are parsed correctly, preserving accurate minute and second #7897 
This PR addresses an issue with the parse_time function where it incorrectly parses time strings with a 4-digit minute and 2-digit second format, leading to the dropping of seconds when the input has 6 digits.

Changes:

- Modified the time parsing regex to preserve leading zeros in minutes and seconds.
- Modified the time parsing regex to handle both 4-digit minutes and 6-digit time formats correctly, ensuring that seconds are retained even when the time string includes the minute and second part in a combined format (e.g., "202201021230" vs "20220102123000").
- Ensured consistent behavior when handling time strings with single-digit minutes/seconds (e.g., "09:05" instead of "9:5").

This fix ensures that both formats are parsed correctly, preserving accurate minute and second PR Description

this fixes [#7897](https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7897)
https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/blob/8c35f7e0fdf46d77faaaa34a1a47e440332326dc/sunpy/time/time.py#L37

user is expected to give proper input as shown in the TIME_FORMAT_LIST 

some of inputs are
 `20140101000001`
`20070504_210812`
`20070504T210812`
`20070504T210812.999999`

in this pr i implemented input validation for parse_time for  above examples

Describe the bug

When I use GONG Ha data recently, I found that the ['rsun_ref'] is missing in the meta data, which causes for example the projection function can not be realized.


### To Reproduce

File C:\ProgramData\anaconda3\Lib\site-packages\sunpy\physics\differential_[rotation.py:597](http://rotation.py:597/), in differential_rotate(smap, observer, time, **diff_rot_kwargs)
594 out_meta.pop(key)
596 # Add a new HGS observer
--> 597 out_meta.update(get_observer_meta(new_observer, out_meta['rsun_ref']*u.m))
599 if is_sub_full_disk:
600 # Define a new reference pixel and the value at the reference pixel.
601 # Note that according to the FITS convention the first pixel in the
602 # image is at (1.0, 1.0).
603 center_rotated = solar_rotate_coordinate(
604 [smap.center](http://smap.center/), observer=new_observer, **diff_rot_kwargs)

File C:\ProgramData\anaconda3\Lib\site-packages\sunpy\util\[metadata.py:167](http://metadata.py:167/), in MetaDict.getitem(self, key)
163 def getitem(self, key):
164 """
165 Override [] indexing.
166 """
--> 167 return OrderedDict.getitem(self, key.lower())

KeyError: 'rsun_ref'

Describe the bug

When I use GONG Ha data recently, I found that the ['rsun_ref'] is missing in the meta data, which causes for example the projection function can not be realized.


### To Reproduce

File C:\ProgramData\anaconda3\Lib\site-packages\sunpy\physics\differential_[rotation.py:597](http://rotation.py:597/), in differential_rotate(smap, observer, time, **diff_rot_kwargs)
594 out_meta.pop(key)
596 # Add a new HGS observer
--> 597 out_meta.update(get_observer_meta(new_observer, out_meta['rsun_ref']*u.m))
599 if is_sub_full_disk:
600 # Define a new reference pixel and the value at the reference pixel.
601 # Note that according to the FITS convention the first pixel in the
602 # image is at (1.0, 1.0).
603 center_rotated = solar_rotate_coordinate(
604 [smap.center](http://smap.center/), observer=new_observer, **diff_rot_kwargs)

File C:\ProgramData\anaconda3\Lib\site-packages\sunpy\util\[metadata.py:167](http://metadata.py:167/), in MetaDict.getitem(self, key)
163 def getitem(self, key):
164 """
165 Override [] indexing.
166 """
--> 167 return OrderedDict.getitem(self, key.lower())

KeyError: 'rsun_ref'

## PR Description
This PR resolves the issue of missing rsun_ref in GONG H-alpha data by ensuring that the rsun_ref metadata is consistently handled across the codebase. If the rsun_ref is missing, it is now handled gracefully, preventing errors and maintaining flexibility in the processing of solar data.

The changes made include:

Metadata Handling: The rsun_ref value is retrieved from the map metadata, and if it is missing, a default behavior is applied instead of raising an error.

This change ensures that the code is more robust and flexible, especially when dealing with solar data that may not always include rsun_ref.

## PR Description
This PR resolves the issue of missing rsun_ref in GONG H-alpha data by ensuring that the rsun_ref metadata is consistently handled across the codebase. If the rsun_ref is missing, it is now handled gracefully, preventing errors and maintaining flexibility in the processing of solar data.

The changes made include:

Metadata Handling: The rsun_ref value is retrieved from the map metadata, and if it is missing, a default behavior is applied instead of raising an error.

This change ensures that the code is more robust and flexible, especially when dealing with solar data that may not always include rsun_ref.

Issue Reference: #7951
